title: utils: close a couple of ReadClosers
body:
`utils.Tar()` and `utils.TarWithChroot()` both return `ReadCloser`s, but when we called them from `utils.TarToFilesystem()` and `utils.TarChrootToFilesystem()` respectively, they were not being closed.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I had started looking at places that call the archive package's Tar() function to archive the contents of directories during [a discussion in a storage PR](https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1765#discussion_r1408384976).  This happened to be the first one, and having forgotten to Close() ReadClosers plenty of times myself, I guess I'm a little sensitive to it.
